data.content:
This is an **Android tombstone** crash log from a .NET MAUI app using the **Mono runtime** (`libmonosgen-2.0.so`). The apple-crash-symbolication skill is for Apple platforms only, so I'll handle this manually.

Let me identify the frames and attempt to fetch debug symbols.

arguments.command: which addr2line llvm-addr2line llvm-symbolizer 2>/dev/null; echo "---"; which curl wget 2>/dev/null

# Try to download debug symbols from the Microsoft symbol server
# ELF BuildId: 1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be
BUILDID="1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be"

# Try the standard symstore ELF convention
echo "=== Trying ELF debug info download ==="
curl -sSL -o /dev/null -w "HTTP %{http_code}: %{url_effective}\n" \
  "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/libmonosgen-2.0.so/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/libmonosgen-2.0.so" 2>&1

curl -sSL -o /dev/null -w "HTTP %{http_code}: %{url_effective}\n" \
  "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/_.debug/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/_.debug" 2>&1

# Try the debuginfod approach
echo "=== Trying debuginfod ==="
curl -sSL -o /dev/null -w "HTTP %{http_code}: %{url_effective}\n" \
  "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/libmonosgen-2.0.so.dbg/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/libmonosgen-2.0.so.dbg" 2>&1

# symweb / NuGet symbols approach
echo "=== Trying alternative paths ==="
curl -sSL -o /dev/null -w "HTTP %{http_code}: %{url_effective}\n" \
  "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/libmonosgen-2.0.so/${BUILDID}/libmonosgen-2.0.so" 2>&1

data.reasoningText:
This is an Android tombstone/crash log, not an Apple .ips crash log. The apple-crash-symbolication skill is specifically for Apple platforms (iOS, tvOS, Mac Catalyst, macOS), so it won't help here.

Let me analyze this crash log:

1. **Platform**: Android (arm64)
2. **App**: `com.companyname.MauiTest` — a .NET MAUI app
3. **Signal**: SIGABRT with abort message "not compatible"
4. **Runtime**: Mono (`libmonosgen-2.0.so`)

The .NET runtime frames are in `libmonosgen-2.0.so` with BuildId `1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be`.


The crash stack shows six frames all within the Mono runtime library, starting from the abort point and working back through the call chain.

I need to get the debug symbols for `libmonosgen-2.0.so` using its BuildId to symbolicate these addresses. The Microsoft symbol server handles ELF binaries differently than Mach-O, typically using the build ID in the URL pattern to locate the corresponding debug information. https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/libmonosgen-2.0.so/elf-buildid-sym-1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be/libmonosgen-2.0.so or the debug info variant. Let me try downloading these symbols and using tools like llvm-addr2line to symbolicate the crash, though this might fail if the specific build wasn't published. I'll start by attempting a few different approaches to get the symbols.

arguments.command: which addr2line llvm-addr2line llvm-symbolizer 2>/dev/null; echo "---"; which curl wget 2>/dev/null

# Try to download debug symbols from the Microsoft symbol server
# ELF BuildId: 1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be
BUILDID="1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be"

# Try the standard symstore ELF convention
echo "=== Trying ELF debug info download ==="
curl -sSL -o /dev/null -w "HTTP %{http_code}: %{url_effective}\n" \
  "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/libmonosgen-2.0.so/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/libmonosgen-2.0.so" 2>&1

curl -sSL -o /dev/null -w "HTTP %{http_code}: %{url_effective}\n" \
  "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/_.debug/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/_.debug" 2>&1

# Try the debuginfod approach
echo "=== Trying debuginfod ==="
curl -sSL -o /dev/null -w "HTTP %{http_code}: %{url_effective}\n" \
  "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/libmonosgen-2.0.so.dbg/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/libmonosgen-2.0.so.dbg" 2>&1

# symweb / NuGet symbols approach
echo "=== Trying alternative paths ==="
curl -sSL -o /dev/null -w "HTTP %{http_code}: %{url_effective}\n" \
  "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/libmonosgen-2.0.so/${BUILDID}/libmonosgen-2.0.so" 2>&1

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "which addr2line llvm-addr2line llvm-symbolizer 2>/dev/null; echo \"---\"; which curl wget 2>/dev/null", "description": "Check available symbolication tools"}

# ELF BuildId: 1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be

# Try the standard symstore ELF convention

curl -sSL -o /dev/null -w "HTTP %{http_code}: %{url_effective}\n" \
  "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/_.debug/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/_.debug" 2>&1

# Try the debuginfod approach

curl -sSL -o /dev/null -w "HTTP %{http_code}: %{url_effective}\n" \
  "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/libmonosgen-2.0.so.dbg/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/libmonosgen-2.0.so.dbg" 2>&1

# symweb / NuGet symbols approach
echo "=== Trying alternative paths ==="

data.content: Debug symbols downloaded and verified (BuildID matches). Now let me symbolicate all frames.

arguments.command:
cd /tmp/sv-ce690c53b959447480611f820408c8c3

echo "=== Symbolicated .NET Runtime (Mono) Frames ==="
echo ""

ADDRS=(0x222098 0x2241ac 0x1abc80 0x1abd14 0x16dfa4 0x16db6c 0x16de04)
FRAMES=(1 2 3 4 5 6 7)

for i in "${!ADDRS[@]}"; do
  RESULT=$(addr2line -C -f -e libmonosgen-2.0.so.debug "${ADDRS[$i]}" 2>&1)
  FUNC=$(echo "$RESULT" | head -1)
  LOC=$(echo "$RESULT" | tail -1)
  printf "#%02d pc %-12s  %s\n                       %s\n\n" "${FRAMES[$i]}" "${ADDRS[$i]}" "$FUNC" "$LOC"
done
